editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Make the Shincheonji-PPP Forced Entry Investigation a Chance for the Party to Regain Its Conscience
summaryTr: Lee Man-hee, chairman of Shincheonji, has been arrested on suspicion of coercing followers to join the People Power Party.
He is suspected of mobilizing church members ahead of the 2021 presidential primary and the 2024 general election.
Observers say the party should use this as an opportunity to set its principles straight.
editorials.bodyTr: The investigation into Shincheonji's coercion of members to join the People Power Party should serve as an opportunity for the party to find its proper place. Lee Man-hee, chairman of Shincheonji's general assembly, was arrested on the 24th on suspicion of forcing believers to join the People Power Party. The chairman is suspected of forcing church members to join the People Power Party ahead of the 2021 People Power Party presidential primary election and the 2024 general election.

editorial.title: [사설] 감시 무풍지대 선관위의 끝 모를 도덕적 해이
editorial.body: 입력 2026.06.18 10:33 2026.06.18 09:43 2026.06.18 06:09 노태악 당시 중앙선거관리위원장이 지난 5일 ‘투표용지 부족 사태’ 관련 대국민 사과를 하고 있다. 뉴스1 중앙선거관리위원회가 투표용지 부족 사태를 초래한 6·3 지방선거 당시 일부 투표소에서 극심한 혼란상이 나타났던 것으로 확인되고 있다. 선거 당일 상황을 기록한 투표록에 따르면, 서울 잠실2동 한 투표소에선 투표관리관 도장이 찍히지 않은 채 용지가 배부됐다. 다른 투표소에선 바닥에 투표용지가 떨어져 있거나, 용지 부족 때문에 선관위에 전화를 걸어도 연락이 닿지 않았다고 한다. 추가 투표용지가 도착한 뒤에도 대기표를 받았던 주민들이 돌아오지 않아 참정권 행사가 불발됐다. 지난해 대선 때도 다른 사람의 선거인명부에 서명하고 투표한 사례가 2000건이 넘는 등 선거 관리 전반의 부실이 심각하다. 다 이유가 있었다. 선거로 업무가 바빠지는 해에 휴직자가 오히려 늘어나는 게 이번뿐 아니라 선관위의 전통이라고 한다. 이 조직이 공정한 선거 관리를 맡을 자격이 있는지 의문을 갖게 한다. 문제는 부실에서 그치지 않는다. 선관위 지도부부터 심각한 도덕적 해이에 빠져 있었다는 사실이 속속 드러나고 있다. 노태악 전 중앙선관위원장은 지난해 덴마크·스웨덴을 비롯해 재임 중 세 차례 간 해외 출장에 모두 배우자를 동반했다. 1200만원이 넘는 비즈니스 항공권 등의 경비를 선관위가 지불했다. 공식적인 외교나 국가 중대사도 아닌 해외 선거 관련 방문에 세금을 흥청망청 쓴 것이어서 외유성 출장이라는 비판을 면키 어렵다. 외부에 공개되는 출장 보고서에 ‘부부 동반’ 문구를 넣지 않은 것만 봐도 명백한 특혜가 아닐 수 없다. 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다. 선관위 직원들은 몰디브 등 세계적인 휴양지로 출장을 가고 선거 홍보물로 활용된다며 해변 사진을 버젓이 보고서에 첨부했다. 역량 강화를 이유로 이탈리아 등 관광지에도 매년 직원을 보냈다. 심지어 선관위 고위직들의 ‘자녀 특혜 채용’ 의혹이 불거진 시기에도 외유성 출장이 계속될 정도로 내부 기강 해이가 심각하다. 독립성을 방패 삼아 외부의 감시와 견제를 피해 성역을 구축해 온 선관위는 전면적인 개혁 없이는 국민의 신뢰를 회복할 수 없는 지경에 이르렀다. 예산 집행의 투명성 확보와 함께 채용 비리를 차단할 감시 시스템이 도입돼야 한다. 신뢰를 잃은 선관위가 관리하는 선거는 결과에 대한 불신의 씨앗을 남길 우려가 있다.
editorial.pullQuote: 기관장이 이러니 직원들의 일탈은 예고된 것이나 마찬가지였다.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: The Election Commission's Bottomless Moral Hazard in the Surveillance Gray Zone
summaryTr: Severe chaos erupted during the June 3 local elections due to ballot shortages, revealing serious negligence in election commission management
Former Chairman Noh Tae-ak wasted over 12 million won in taxpayer money on overseas business trips accompanied by his spouse
The election commission has evaded oversight by using independence as a shield, and trust cannot be restored without comprehensive reform
editorial.bodyTr: The National Election Commission has confirmed that severe chaos occurred at some polling places during the June 3 local elections that led to the ballot shortage crisis. According to voting records documenting the situation on election day, at one polling station in Jamsil 2-dong, Seoul, ballots were distributed without the election supervisor's stamp. At other polling stations, ballots were found scattered on the floor, and residents reported that calls to the election commission went unanswered due to ballot shortages. Even after additional ballots arrived, residents who had received waiting tickets did not return, resulting in lost voting opportunities. During last year's presidential election, there were over 2,000 cases of people signing other voters' names and casting ballots, indicating serious mismanagement across the entire election administration system. There were reasons for this. According to reports, it is a tradition at the election commission that the number of employees on leave actually increases during years when election work becomes busy. This raises questions about whether this organization is qualified to manage fair elections. The problem extends beyond mere negligence. It is becoming increasingly clear that the election commission's leadership has fallen into serious ethical lapses. Former Election Commission Chairman Roh Tae-ak accompanied his spouse on all three overseas business trips during his tenure, including visits to Denmark and Sweden. The election commission paid for business-class airfare exceeding 12 million won and other expenses. Using taxpayer money for overseas visits related to elections that were neither official diplomatic missions nor major state affairs is difficult to defend against criticism of junket travel. The fact that official trip reports did not mention "accompanied by spouse" makes clear that this was outright favoritism. Given the conduct of the agency head, employee misconduct was virtually inevitable. Election commission staff went on business trips to world-renowned vacation destinations like the Maldives and brazenly attached beach photos to reports, claiming they would be used in election promotional materials. They sent employees yearly to tourist destinations like Italy under the guise of capacity building. Internal discipline has deteriorated so severely that even when suspicions arose of "nepotistic hiring" among senior election commission officials, junket travel continued unabated. The election commission, using independence as a shield to avoid external oversight and checks while creating a sanctuary, has reached a point where it cannot recover public trust without comprehensive reform. Transparency in budget execution must be secured, and a monitoring system to prevent hiring irregularities must be introduced. Elections managed by an election commission that has lost public trust risk leaving seeds of doubt about their outcomes.

leanScore.label: Lean progressive
leanScore.rationale: 공공기관의 도덕적 해이와 특권, 투명성 부족을 강하게 비판하며 감시 체계 강화를 요구하는 진보적 프레이밍으로, 권력 기관의 부실과 기강 해이에 대한 진보진영의 전형적 관점을 드러냄.

editorial.title: Clean house at KFA
editorial.body: Korea's worst World Cup result exposes flaws in soccer association's leadership The nation had high expectations for the national team heading into the opening game of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. South Korea fielded what was widely regarded as its strongest-ever squad, led by world-class players such as Son Heung-min, Kim Min-jae and Lee Kang-in. Group A, which included South Korea, was considered relatively weak except for co-host Mexico. However, at this World Cup, which for the first time featured an expanded 48-team field with 32 teams progressing to the next stage, South Korea failed to make it past the first hurdle, finishing 34th overall. For South Korea, which has qualified for 11 consecutive World Cups since 1986, it was its worst-ever World Cup performance. In the previous World Cup finals that South Korea had qualified for, 32 nations competed. Above all, South Korea's third group-stage match against South Africa was an abysmal display — arguably the worst performance in the country's World Cup history. The public was greatly disappointed by the Korean team's defeat. It cannot be said categorically that the defeat was caused by the head coach and the Korea Football Association. Nevertheless, it is undeniable that there were major shortcomings in the coach's strategy, leadership and handling of the team, as well as in the KFA's administration and systems supporting everything from player scouting to the management of the national team. Head coach Hong Myung-bo made the baffling decision to leave captain Son Heung-min out of the starting lineup against South Africa. Hong was disappointing in almost every respect, including player motivation, opponent-specific tactics and in-game crisis management. The Korean players looked strangely lethargic, and even as defeat loomed, Hong did not switch to a more attacking approach. His abilities as head coach of the South Korean national team had already been tested once before the 2026 FIFA World Cup. When he was in charge of the national team at the 2014 Brazil World Cup, South Korea was eliminated in the group stage after posting a dismal record of one draw and two defeats. At the time, the KFA's white paper cited "unchanging tactics and a lack of strategies for responding to different game situations" as reasons for the team's failure, and the same problems resurfaced at the 2026 World Cup. Despite his shortcomings as a coach, which had already been exposed at the 2014 World Cup, the KFA rehired Hong as head coach of the national team in 2024, choosing him over numerous foreign candidates even though he had not gone through the proper interview process. As controversy mounted over the fairness of his appointment, the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism conducted an audit of the KFA, which revealed violations of the appointment procedures. Despite the many problems that had come to light and the ministry's recommendation that the KFA impose severe disciplinary measures on its leadership, including President Chung Mong-gyu, Chung ran for a fourth consecutive term as KFA president in February last year and won by an overwhelming margin. Around the time of Hong's appointment and Chung's reelection, criticism mounted that the extended Hyundai family was maintaining its grip on the KFA and that graduates of a particular university had monopolized key positions within the association, forming a cartel that fostered abuses of power and undemocratic decision-making. The KFA turned a deaf ear to outside criticism of the cartel, dogmatically kept Hong Myung-bo in charge, and ended up posting disastrous results. Given that the popularity of the national team has long sustained the Korean soccer ecosystem, this World Cup elimination can only be seen as a grave warning sign for Korean soccer. Chung had already announced that he would step down as KFA president after this World Cup. Hong Myung-bo officially resigned on Monday, taking responsibility for South Korea's group-stage elimination. Their resignations should serve as an opportunity to accelerate self-reflection and reform aimed at uprooting the cartel and dogma within the KFA. summary: South Korea's worst World Cup result exposed systemic failures in KFA leadership and strategy.
Coach Hong Myung-bo repeated 2014 mistakes, benching Son Heung-min against South Africa disastrously.
KFA president Chung Mong-gyu ignored audit findings and nepotism concerns, perpetuating organizational dysfunction.
editorial.stance: Institutional incompetence and entrenched power structures at the KFA must be dismantled for Korean soccer's recovery.
summaryTr: 한국의 최악의 월드컵 성적은 대한축구협회 지도부의 체계적 실패와 전략적 오류를 드러냈다.
홍명보 감독은 2014년의 실수를 반복해 남아프리카전에서 손흥민을 벤치에 앉혀 참담한 결과를 초래했다.
정몽규 대한축구협회장은 감시 결과와 연고주의 우려를 무시하며 조직의 기능 부전을 지속시켰다.
editorial.bodyTr:
한국 축구의 최악의 월드컵 성적이 축구협회 지도부의 결함을 드러내다

국가대표팀은 2026년 FIFA 월드컵 개막전을 앞두고 높은 기대감을 갖고 있었다. 한국은 손흥민, 김민재, 이강인 같은 세계적 수준의 선수들이 이끄는 역대 최강으로 평가받는 대표팀을 꾸렸다. 한국이 속한 A조는 공동개최국인 멕시코를 제외하고는 비교적 약한 팀들로 구성되었다. 하지만 처음으로 48개 팀이 참가하고 32개 팀이 다음 단계로 진출하는 형식으로 확대된 이번 월드컵에서 한국은 첫 번째 관문을 통과하지 못했고, 최종 34위로 탈락했다. 1986년 이후 11회 연속 월드컵 본선 진출국인 한국에게 있어 역대 최악의 월드컵 성적이었다. 이전 월드컵들에서는 32개국이 참가했다. 무엇보다 남아프리카공화국과의 3차 조별리그전에서 한국은 한심한 경기력을 보였으며, 이는 국가대표팀 월드컵 역사상 최악의 성능이라 할 수 있다. 국민들은 대표팀의 패배에 크게 실망했다. 이 패배가 홍명보 감독과 대한축구협회로 인한 것이라고 단정할 수는 없다. 그럼에도 불구하고 감독의 전술, 리더십, 팀 운영에서 그리고 선수 영입부터 국가대표팀 관리에 이르기까지 모든 것을 지원하는 축협의 행정과 체계에 큰 허점이 있었다는 점은 부인할 수 없다.

홍명보 감독은 남아프리카공화국과의 경기에서 주장인 손흥민을 선발 라인업에서 제외하는 당황스러운 결정을 내렸다. 홍 감독은 선수 동기 부여, 상대팀별 맞춤 전술, 경기 위기 관리 등 거의 모든 측면에서 실망스러웠다. 한국 선수들은 묘하게 무기력해 보였으며, 패배가 임박해도 홍 감독은 더욱 공격적인 접근 방식으로 전환하지 않았다. 그의 한국 국가대표팀 감독으로서의 능력은 2026년 월드컵 이전에 한 번 이미 검증된 바 있다. 2014년 브라질 월드컵 당시 국가대표팀을 지휘했을 때 한국은 무승부 1경기와 패배 2경기의 부진한 성적으로 조별리그에서 탈락했다. 당시 축협이 발간한 백서는 팀의 실패 원인으로 "불변하는 전술과 다양한 게임 상황에 대응하는 전략 부족"을 지적했으며, 같은 문제들이 2026년 월드컵에서 재차 나타났다.

2014년 월드컵에서 이미 드러난 감독으로서의 단점에도 불구하고, 축협은 2024년 홍명보를 국가대표팀 감독으로 재임용했다. 수많은 외국인 후보를 제치고 홍을 선택했으며, 심지어 정해진 인터뷰 절차를 거치지도 않았다. 임용의 공정성을 놓고 논란이 커지자 문화체육관광부는 축협을 감시했고, 그 결과 임용 절차의 위반이 드러났다. 밝혀진 많은 문제점과 정몽규 회장을 포함한 축협 지도부에 대한 엄격한 징계 조치를 권고한 부처의 의견에도 불구하고, 정몽규는 지난해 2월 축협 회장 4연임에 나섰고 압도적인 표차로 당선되었다.

홍의 임용 시점과 정의 재선 시점 무렵, 현대 일족이 축협에 대한 장악력을 유지하고 있다는 비판과 특정 대학 출신자들이 축협 내 주요 직책을 독점하고 있으며 권력 남용과 비민주적 의사결정을 조장하는 카르텔을 형성하고 있다는 비판이 증가했다. 축협은 외부의 카르텔 비판에 귀를 닫고, 교조적으로 홍명보를 유지했으며, 결국 참담한 결과를 맞게 되었다. 국가대표팀의 인기가 오랫동안 한국 축구 생태계를 지탱해온 점을 감안할 때, 이번 월드컵 탈락은 한국 축구에 대한 중대한 경고 신호라 할 수밖에 없다. 정몽규는 이미 이번 월드컵 이후 축협 회장직에서 물러나겠다고 발표한 바 있다. 홍명보는 월요일 공식 사임서를 제출했고, 한국의 조별리그 탈락에 책임을 졌다. 이들의 사임은 축협 내 카르텔과 교조적 태도를 뿌리 뽑기 위한 자기 성찰과 개혁을 가속화할 수 있는 기회가 되어야 한다.

editorials.title: Clean house at KFA
editorials.body: Korea's worst World Cup result exposes flaws in soccer association's leadership The nation had high expectations for the national team heading into the opening game of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. South Korea fielded what was widely regarded as its strongest-ever squad, led by world-class players such as Son Heung-min, Kim Min-jae and Lee Kang-in. Group A, which included South Korea, was considered relatively weak except for co-host Mexico. However, at this World Cup, which for the first time featured an expanded 48-team field with 32 teams progressing to the next stage, South Korea failed to make it past the first hurdle, finishing 34th overall. For South Korea, which has qualified for 11 consecutive World Cups since 1986, it was its worst-ever World Cup performance. In the previous World Cup finals that South Korea had qualified for, 32 nations competed. Above all, South Korea's third group-stage match against South Africa was an abysmal display — arguably the worst performance in the country's World Cup history. The public was greatly disappointed by the Korean team's defeat. It cannot be said categorically that the defeat was caused by the head coach and the Korea Football Association. Nevertheless, it is undeniable that there were major shortcomings in the coach's strategy, leadership and handling of the team, as well as in the KFA's administration and systems supporting everything from player scouting to the management of the national team. Head coach Hong Myung-bo made the baffling decision to leave captain Son Heung-min out of the starting lineup against South Africa. Hong was disappointing in almost every respect, including player motivation, opponent-specific tactics and in-game crisis management. The Korean players looked strangely lethargic, and even as defeat loomed, Hong did not switch to a more attacking approach. His abilities as head coach of the South Korean national team had already been tested once before the 2026 FIFA World Cup. When he was in charge of the national team at the 2014 Brazil World Cup, South Korea was eliminated in the group stage after posting a dismal record of one draw and two defeats. At the time, the KFA's white paper cited "unchanging tactics and a lack of strategies for responding to different game situations" as reasons for the team's failure, and the same problems resurfaced at the 2026 World Cup. Despite his shortcomings as a coach, which had already been exposed at the 2014 World Cup, the KFA rehired Hong as head coach of the national team in 2024, choosing him over numerous foreign candidates even though he had not gone through the proper interview process. As controversy mounted over the fairness of his appointment, the Ministry of Culture, Sports and Tourism conducted an audit of the KFA, which revealed violations of the appointment procedures. Despite the many problems that had come to light and the ministry's recommendation that the KFA impose severe disciplinary measures on its leadership, including President Chung Mong-gyu, Chung ran for a fourth consecutive term as KFA president in February last year and won by an overwhelming margin. Around the time of Hong's appointment and Chung's reelection, criticism mounted that the extended Hyundai family was maintaining its grip on the KFA and that graduates of a particular university had monopolized key positions within the association, forming a cartel that fostered abuses of power and undemocratic decision-making. The KFA turned a deaf ear to outside criticism of the cartel, dogmatically kept Hong Myung-bo in charge, and ended up posting disastrous results. Given that the popularity of the national team has long sustained the Korean soccer ecosystem, this World Cup elimination can only be seen as a grave warning sign for Korean soccer. Chung had already announced that he would step down as KFA president after this World Cup. Hong Myung-bo officially resigned on Monday, taking responsibility for South Korea's group-stage elimination. Their resignations should serve as an opportunity to accelerate self-reflection and reform aimed at uprooting the cartel and dogma within the KFA. 홍명보 감독은 2014년의 실수를 반복해 남아프리카전에서 손흥민을 벤치에 앉혀 참담한 결과를 초래했다.
정몽규 대한축구협회장은 감시 결과와 연고주의 우려를 무시하며 조직의 기능 부전을 지속시켰다.

editorial.titleTr: Editorial: Three Mega Projects Must Become Catalyst for National Leap Forward
summaryTr: Government Announces 1.5 Quadrillion Won Investment in Semiconductors, AI, and Physical AI
Bold plan to develop local regions as advanced industry hubs
A critical moment to seize as an opportunity for national leapfrog growth
editorial.bodyTr:
The government unveiled its "three mega projects" on the 29th, investing approximately 1,500 trillion won in semiconductors, artificial intelligence data centers, and physical AI. While the unprecedented scale of investment deserves attention, the plan to develop regional areas as key hubs for cutting-edge industries in the artificial intelligence era is particularly striking. This project represents a significant departure from Seoul-centric development policies that have long dominated the nation's economic landscape.

The regional development component of these mega projects is noteworthy because it seeks to balance national growth by distributing advanced industrial clusters beyond the capital region. By establishing AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing facilities in provincial areas, the government aims to create new economic engines outside of Seoul and its surrounding regions.

This approach addresses longstanding regional disparities that have plagued Korea's development. For too long, the concentration of high-tech industries in the capital region has exacerbated inequalities between Seoul and provincial cities. The mega projects offer an opportunity to reverse this trend and foster more equitable regional growth.

However, the success of these initiatives will depend on effective implementation and sustained commitment. Simply allocating large sums of money is insufficient without proper infrastructure, skilled workforce development, and coordinated cooperation between central and local governments. The authorities must ensure that regional areas receive not just investment capital but also the support systems necessary to attract and retain talent.

If executed well, these mega projects could reshape Korea's economic geography and establish a more balanced development model for the future.

editorials.titleTr: Editorial: Three Mega Projects Must Become Catalyst for National Leap Forward
summaryTr: Government Announces 1.5 Quadrillion Won Investment in Semiconductors, AI, and Physical AI
Bold plan to develop local regions as advanced industry hubs
A critical moment to seize as an opportunity for national leapfrog growth
editorials.bodyTr:
The government unveiled its "three mega projects" on the 29th, investing approximately 1,500 trillion won in semiconductors, artificial intelligence data centers, and physical AI. While the unprecedented scale of investment deserves attention, the plan to develop regional areas as key hubs for cutting-edge industries in the artificial intelligence era is particularly striking. This project represents a significant departure from Seoul-centric development policies that have long dominated the nation's economic landscape.

The regional development component of these mega projects is noteworthy because it seeks to balance national growth by distributing advanced industrial clusters beyond the capital region. By establishing AI data centers and semiconductor manufacturing facilities in provincial areas, the government aims to create new economic engines outside of Seoul and its surrounding regions.

This approach addresses longstanding regional disparities that have plagued Korea's development. For too long, the concentration of high-tech industries in the capital region has exacerbated inequalities between Seoul and provincial cities. The mega projects offer an opportunity to reverse this trend and foster more equitable regional growth.

However, the success of these initiatives will depend on effective implementation and sustained commitment. Simply allocating large sums of money is insufficient without proper infrastructure, skilled workforce development, and coordinated cooperation between central and local governments. The authorities must ensure that regional areas receive not just investment capital but also the support systems necessary to attract and retain talent.

If executed well, these mega projects could reshape Korea's economic geography and establish a more balanced development model for the future.

editorial.titleTr: Honam Semiconductor Project: Controversial but a National Imperative [Editorial]
summaryTr: The Honam semiconductor project is controversial, but it must succeed for the nation.
The semiconductor industry is a core competitive advantage for the Korean economy, and regional balanced development is necessary.
It is a strategic task that the government and companies must pursue together without failure.

leanScore.label: Lean conservative
leanScore.rationale: 정부-기업 협력, 국가 경쟁력 강화, 전략적 산업 육성이라는 보수적 프레임으로 대규모 국책사업을 옹호하며, 시장과 기업 주도의 경제 성장 논리를 강조함

editorials.title: 호남 반도체, 논란 있지만 실패해선 안 될 국가적 과제 [사설]
editorials.body: 호남 반도체, 논란 있지만 실패해선 안 될 국가적 과제 [사설] , 오피니언
editorials.pullQuote: 호남 반도체, 논란 있지만 실패해선 안 될 국가적 과제
summary: 호남 반도체 사업은 논란이 있지만 국가적으로 반드시 성공해야 한다.
반도체 산업은 한국 경제의 핵심 경쟁력으로 지역 균형 발전이 필요하다.
정부와 기업이 함께 실패 없이 추진해야 할 전략적 과제다.
editorials.stance: 호남 반도체 사업의 논쟁을 인정하면서도 국가 경쟁력 강화를 위해 반드시 성공해야 한다는 입장
